sentence_reviews.text: Executable and analyzable program structures support code generation, verification, and tool use.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA programming/agent/tool reasoning context; Formal Methods for ML survey for verification context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Program structures are clearly executable/analyzable, and verification is supported by formal-methods context. Code generation and tool use need more specific sources for exact verification.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations is to connect Programming languages, compilers & type systems, Algorithms & data structures to downstream capabilities including Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA algorithmic/problem-solving and logic material; Formal Methods for ML survey for program/verification context.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency side is plausible, but several downstream capabilities are graph-derived and not directly evidenced by the cited references.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports symbolic reasoning/program-like representations generally, but not the full neuro-symbolic and physics-informed claim.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited packet sources do not directly support this specific downstream relationship.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Programming languages, compilers & type systems, Algorithms & data structures.

sentence_reviews.notes: This is generic generated limitation text and not directly evidenced for programs/ASTs/IR.

sentence_reviews.text: Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.

edge_reviews.notes: Programs, ASTs, bytecode, and IRs are compiler/programming-language artifacts; requires/core is supported.
Algorithms and data structures are common prerequisites for analyzing and executing program representations, but the cited source is broad.
